commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: fix(agents): bound body-less MCP HTTP text responses
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`ensureGlobalFetchResponse` in the MCP HTTP fetch wrapper reads the entire response body via `await response.text()` for body-less `Response`-like objects. This path exists so the MCP SDK can parse OAuth error text from foreign response implementations (introduced in #91451), but it has no size limit. A buggy or malicious peer that returns a body-less response with an oversized `text()` payload can force the runtime to buffer the whole string into memory — an OOM vector. This is the same class of boundary resilience issue fixed for provider response reads by Alix-007 in #96903, #96886, and similar.

## Changes

- `src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.ts`: added `MCP_HTTP_MAX_TEXT_RESPONSE_BYTES = 1 * 1024 * 1024`. `ensureGlobalFetchResponse` now:
  - checks `Content-Length` before calling `response.text()` and returns a null body if it exceeds the cap;
  - checks the returned text length after the call and returns a null body if it exceeds the cap;
  - preserves the existing small-OAuth-error-body behavior below the cap.
- `src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.test.ts`: added a regression test that uses the same `ForeignResponse` pattern from #91451 but with a 1 MiB + 1 byte `text()` payload, asserting the response body is dropped.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: A body-less MCP HTTP response with an oversized `text()` payload must not be buffered whole; the wrapper must drop the body while preserving status/headers.
- **Real environment tested**: Local Node v22 workspace, using the exported production `buildMcpHttpFetch` against a mock undici runtime that returns a `ForeignResponse`-like object.
- **Exact steps**: `node --import tsx /tmp/proof-mcp-http-fetch.mts` constructs a body-less 400 response whose `text()` returns 1 MiB + 1 byte, calls `buildMcpHttpFetch(...)(...)` and prints the resulting status and body presence.
- **Observed result after fix**: `status=400 body=null`. Before the fix the same script printed `status=400 body=present`, meaning the oversized text was being forwarded into the returned `Response`.
- **What was not tested**: A live MCP OAuth server was not hit; the proof uses the same `ForeignResponse` pattern the existing regression test uses, and the in-repo Vitest locks both the happy path and the oversized path.

## Evidence

Terminal proof (real production `buildMcpHttpFetch`, oversized `ForeignResponse`):

```
$ node --import tsx /tmp/proof-mcp-http-fetch.mts
status=400 body=null
```

Negative control (temporary revert to `origin/main`):

```
$ git checkout origin/main -- src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.ts
$ node --import tsx /tmp/proof-mcp-http-fetch.mts
status=400 body=present
$ git checkout HEAD -- src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.ts
```

Focused Vitest:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.test.ts --run

Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  11 passed (11)
   Duration  2.50s
```

Type checks:

```
$ pnpm tsgo:core      # exit 0
$ pnpm tsgo:core:test # exit 0
```
target.author: pick-cat
